footer.subtitle: Holiday home in Cabo de Gata

faq.title: We answer your questions about Cabo Indalo
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know before booking your accommodation in Cabo de Gata.

location.q: Where exactly is Cabo Indalo located?
location.a: Cabo Indalo is at Calle Poseidonia 14, in Cabo de Gata (Almeria), inside the Cabo de Gata-Nijar Natural Park. It is a quiet residential area, very close to the Cabo de Gata salt flats and with easy access to the best beaches in the natural park.

beach.q: How far is the nearest beach?
beach.a: Cabo de Gata beach is just a 5-minute walk from the house. It is a wide beach, ideal for families, with crystal-clear waters. In addition, unspoilt beaches like Los Genoveses or Monsul are 15-18 minutes away by car.

pets.q: Are pets allowed at the accommodation?
pets.a: Yes! Cabo Indalo is a pet-friendly accommodation. We know your pet is part of the family, so they are welcome at no extra cost. We only ask you to take care of shared spaces and cleanliness.

amenities.q: Is there WiFi, air conditioning, and heating?
amenities.a: Yes, the house has high-speed WiFi for work or streaming, air conditioning on the main floor, and ceiling fans in each bedroom.

parking.q: Is it easy to park near the house?
parking.a: Yes, it is usually easy to find parking near the house. It is true that in high season it can be a bit more difficult, but in general you should not have problems. You will not need a garage or a reserved spot.

shops.q: Are there supermarkets and restaurants nearby?
shops.a: There is a supermarket just a few minutes away on foot, as well as bakeries, pharmacies, and several bars and restaurants with local tapas and Mediterranean cuisine. For more variety, Almeria city is 25 minutes away by car. Also, in the nearby village of Pujaire, there is a very complete Spar supermarket with all kinds of products.

booking.q: How can I book Cabo Indalo?
booking.a: Bookings are managed through Airbnb, where you can check availability, read reviews from other guests, and book securely. You can also contact us by email or phone to answer any questions before booking.

activities.q: What can I visit in Cabo de Gata Natural Park?
activities.a: Cabo de Gata-Nijar Natural Park offers unspoilt coves such as Genoveses and Monsul, the iconic Arrecife de las Sirenas, hiking routes with sea views, the Cabo de Gata salt flats (a paradise for watching flamingos), and charming villages such as San Jose and Las Negras.

whatToVisit.title: What to see in Cabo de Gata during your stay
whatToVisit.intro: If this is your first visit, this guide helps you discover special places near Cabo Indalo.
whatToVisit.p1: Start with Cabo de Gata salt flats at sunrise or sunset. It is one of the most iconic landscapes in the park and great for flamingo watching.
whatToVisit.p2: Save one morning for Arrecife de las Sirenas and end your day in charming villages like San Jose or Las Negras.
whatToVisit.tip1: Bring water, a hat, and comfortable shoes.
whatToVisit.tip2: Avoid the hottest hours in summer.
whatToVisit.tip3: Check wind conditions before choosing beach or viewpoint.

nearbyBeaches.title: Nearby beaches worth visiting from Cabo Indalo
nearbyBeaches.intro: From the house, you can combine easy daily beach plans with iconic coves in a short drive.
nearbyBeaches.p1: Cabo de Gata beach is a 5-minute walk and perfect for a comfortable family plan any time of day.
nearbyBeaches.p2: For a more natural setting, visit Genoveses and Monsul by car. They are famous for unspoilt landscapes and protected surroundings.
nearbyBeaches.tip1: Arrive early in high season for easier parking.
nearbyBeaches.tip2: Bring umbrella and food for beaches without services.
nearbyBeaches.tip3: Respect protected areas and leave no waste.

howToGetThere.title: How to get to Cabo Indalo and move around
howToGetThere.intro: Here is a clear reference to organize your arrival and save time during your stay.
howToGetThere.p1: If you fly in, Almeria airport is around 15-20 minutes away by car. Renting a car is the most practical option to explore.
howToGetThere.p2: If you come by car, parking near the house is usually easy. In high season it may take longer, but private garage is usually not needed.
howToGetThere.tip1: Save the exact address in Google Maps before departure.
howToGetThere.tip2: Plan groceries in Cabo de Gata or Pujaire.
howToGetThere.tip3: Combine driving days with local walking plans.
